 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "2e7c6603",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Walkthrough - DLinear vs. a Patch Model vs. Classical Baselines, with a Forced Ablation\n",
    "\n",
    "The claim from Chapter 7 Lesson 02 was that on standard long-horizon benchmarks **the protocol\n",
    "explained more of the variance than the architecture did**. Here you make that happen on purpose:\n",
    "the same four models, the same data, and a ranking that flips when we change normalisation,\n",
    "lookback length and evaluation protocol.\n",
    "\n",
    "We stay in numpy/scikit-learn \u2014 DLinear *is* a linear map, and a patch model without attention is\n",
    "enough to make the point. (A real PatchTST would add self-attention over the patch tokens; the\n",
    "patching, normalisation and protocol are what move the numbers.)\n",
    "\n",
    "Companion lessons: [L01](../lessons/L01_sequence_models.md),\n",
    "[L02](../lessons/L02_transformers_and_dlinear.md), [L03](../lessons/L03_trajectory_prediction.md)."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "81284575",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:24:33.075739Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:24:33.075647Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.095267Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.094784Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge\n",
    "from sklearn.neural_network import MLPRegressor\n",
    "\n",
    "RNG = np.random.default_rng(5)\n",
    "plt.rcParams.update({\"figure.figsize\": (11, 3.4), \"axes.grid\": True, \"grid.alpha\": 0.3})\n",
    "\n",
    "H = 48          # forecast horizon\n",
    "M = 24          # dominant season"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b5e6e47f",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 1 - Two datasets with different structure\n",
    "\n",
    "* **`seasonal`** \u2014 strong daily + weekly seasonality on a slow random-walk level. This is what the\n",
    "  long-horizon benchmarks mostly look like, and it is linear-model heaven.\n",
    "* **`regime`** \u2014 the same seasonality, but the amplitude and the level jump between regimes. Now\n",
    "  the mapping from the lookback window to the future is *not* stationary."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "0da1ea76",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.097041Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.096885Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.249283Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.248674Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1200x300 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def make_series(kind, n=6000, seed=0):\n",
    "    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)\n",
    "    t = np.arange(n)\n",
    "    s = 4 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * t / M) + 1.5 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * t / (M * 7) + 0.5)\n",
    "    level = np.cumsum(rng.normal(0, 0.03, n))\n",
    "    if kind == \"seasonal\":\n",
    "        return s + level + rng.normal(0, 0.5, n)\n",
    "    amp = np.ones(n)\n",
    "    lev = np.zeros(n)\n",
    "    for a, b in zip(range(0, n, 700), range(700, n + 700, 700)):     # regimes\n",
    "        amp[a:b] = rng.uniform(0.5, 2.2)\n",
    "        lev[a:b] = rng.normal(0, 3)\n",
    "    return s * amp + lev + level + rng.normal(0, 0.5, n) * amp\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "data = {k: make_series(k, seed=i) for i, k in enumerate([\"seasonal\", \"regime\"])}\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 3))\n",
    "for a, (k, v) in zip(ax, data.items()):\n",
    "    a.plot(v[:1200], lw=0.7); a.set_title(k)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "5075e03b",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 2 - Windowing, with the two protocol knobs we will abuse later\n",
    "\n",
    "`lookback` (how much history each model sees) and `inst_norm` (subtract each window's own mean and\n",
    "std, predict, then add them back \u2014 RevIN in one line). Both are usually reported as\n",
    "implementation details. They are not."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "97b9939b",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.250926Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.250833Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.253538Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.253216Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "def windows(y, lookback, horizon=H):\n",
    "    idx = np.arange(len(y) - lookback - horizon + 1)\n",
    "    X = np.array([y[i:i + lookback] for i in idx])\n",
    "    Y = np.array([y[i + lookback:i + lookback + horizon] for i in idx])\n",
    "    return X, Y\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def norm_fit(X, inst_norm):\n",
    "    if not inst_norm:\n",
    "        return np.zeros(len(X)), np.ones(len(X))\n",
    "    return X.mean(1), X.std(1) + 1e-6"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "39343c06",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 3 - The four models\n",
    "\n",
    "* **Seasonal naive** \u2014 $\\hat y_{t+h}=y_{t+h-M}$. Free, and the baseline everything must beat.\n",
    "* **Linear** \u2014 one ridge regression from the whole lookback window to all $H$ horizons.\n",
    "* **DLinear** \u2014 moving-average decomposition into trend + remainder, one linear map per component,\n",
    "  summed. Note that this is still a *linear* map of the window: the decomposition changes which\n",
    "  directions the ridge penalty shrinks, nothing more. That is worth knowing before attributing a\n",
    "  win to \"the decomposition\".\n",
    "* **PatchMLP** \u2014 split the lookback into non-overlapping patches, take per-patch mean/std/slope as\n",
    "  the token embedding, feed a small MLP. Patching + a nonlinear head, no attention."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "8d90bab0",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.255462Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.254843Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.260454Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.260021Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "def seasonal_naive(Xtr, Ytr, Xte, **kw):\n",
    "    reps = int(np.ceil(H / M))\n",
    "    return np.tile(Xte[:, -M:], (1, reps))[:, :H]\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def linear_model(Xtr, Ytr, Xte, alpha=10.0, **kw):\n",
    "    return Ridge(alpha=alpha).fit(Xtr, Ytr).predict(Xte)\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def dlinear(Xtr, Ytr, Xte, kernel=25, alpha=10.0, **kw):\n",
    "    def decomp(X):\n",
    "        k = np.ones(kernel) / kernel\n",
    "        pad = kernel // 2\n",
    "        trend = np.array([np.convolve(np.pad(x, pad, mode=\"edge\"), k, \"valid\")[:X.shape[1]] for x in X])\n",
    "        return trend, X - trend\n",
    "    Ttr, Str = decomp(Xtr)\n",
    "    Tte, Ste = decomp(Xte)\n",
    "    # one linear map per component, summed - i.e. a single ridge on the decomposed basis.\n",
    "    # (Fitting two ridges to the *same* target and adding them would predict 2y: a real bug, and\n",
    "    #  an easy one to ship.)\n",
    "    return Ridge(alpha=alpha).fit(np.c_[Ttr, Str], Ytr).predict(np.c_[Tte, Ste])\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def patch_embed(X, patch=12):\n",
    "    n_p = X.shape[1] // patch\n",
    "    Xp = X[:, -n_p * patch:].reshape(len(X), n_p, patch)\n",
    "    slope = (Xp[:, :, -1] - Xp[:, :, 0]) / patch\n",
    "    return np.concatenate([Xp.mean(2), Xp.std(2), slope, Xp[:, :, -1]], axis=1)\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def patch_mlp(Xtr, Ytr, Xte, **kw):\n",
    "    m = MLPRegressor(hidden_layer_sizes=(128, 128), max_iter=120, random_state=0,\n",
    "                     early_stopping=True, learning_rate_init=3e-3)\n",
    "    import warnings\n",
    "    warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\")\n",
    "    m.fit(patch_embed(Xtr), Ytr)\n",
    "    return m.predict(patch_embed(Xte))\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "MODELS = {\"seasonal naive\": seasonal_naive, \"linear\": linear_model,\n",
    "          \"DLinear\": dlinear, \"PatchMLP\": patch_mlp}"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "3a7af5d5",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 4 - Two evaluation protocols\n",
    "\n",
    "**Single chronological split** \u2014 the benchmark standard: one train block, one test block, one\n",
    "number. **Rolling origin** \u2014 Chapter 1 Lesson 03: several folds, and a *distribution* of errors."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "1f9f7d0f",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.261721Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.261640Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.266326Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:24:35.265904Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "def evaluate(y, model_fn, lookback, inst_norm, protocol=\"single\", folds=5, metric_scale=\"original\"):\n",
    "    X, Y = windows(y, lookback)\n",
    "    scores = []\n",
    "    splits = ([(int(0.7 * len(X)), len(X))] if protocol == \"single\"\n",
    "              else [(int(len(X) * (0.4 + 0.1 * i)), int(len(X) * (0.5 + 0.1 * i))) for i in range(folds)])\n",
    "    for tr_end, te_end in splits:\n",
    "        Xtr, Ytr = X[:tr_end], Y[:tr_end]\n",
    "        Xte, Yte = X[tr_end:te_end], Y[tr_end:te_end]\n",
    "        mu_tr, sd_tr = norm_fit(Xtr, inst_norm)\n",
    "        mu_te, sd_te = norm_fit(Xte, inst_norm)\n",
    "        pred = model_fn((Xtr - mu_tr[:, None]) / sd_tr[:, None],\n",
    "                        (Ytr - mu_tr[:, None]) / sd_tr[:, None],\n",
    "                        (Xte - mu_te[:, None]) / sd_te[:, None])\n",
    "        pred = pred * sd_te[:, None] + mu_te[:, None]\n",
    "        if metric_scale == \"normalised\":         # score each window in its own units\n",
    "            scores.append(np.sqrt(np.mean(((pred - Yte) / sd_te[:, None]) ** 2)))\n",
    "        else:\n",
    "            scores.append(np.sqrt(np.mean((pred - Yte) ** 2)))\n",
    "    return np.array(scores)\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def table(y, lookback, inst_norm, protocol, metric_scale=\"original\"):\n",
    "    out = {}\n",
    "    for name, fn in MODELS.items():\n",
    "        s = evaluate(y, fn, lookback, inst_norm, protocol, metric_scale=metric_scale)\n",
    "        out[name] = (s.mean(), s.std())\n",
    "    return out\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def show(title, res):\n",
    "    order = sorted(res, key=lambda k: res[k][0])\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n{title}\")\n",
    "    for r, k in enumerate(order, 1):\n",
    "        m, s = res[k]\n",
    "        print(f\"  {r}. {k:<16} RMSE {m:6.3f}\" + (f\" \u00b1 {s:.3f}\" if s > 0 else \"\"))"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "57807233",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [

   ]

    "## Part 8 - The whole grid, in one picture\n",

  {
   "cell_type": "code",

   "id": "98d74697",
   "metadata": {

     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:25:49.833230Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:25:49.832911Z",

   "outputs": [

   "source": [
    "configs = [(\"LB48\\nnorm\", 48, True), (\"LB96\\nnorm\", 96, True), (\"LB336\\nnorm\", 336, True),\n",
    "           (\"LB48\\nraw\", 48, False), (\"LB96\\nraw\", 96, False), (\"LB336\\nraw\", 336, False)]\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4), sharey=False)\n",
    "for a, (dname, y) in zip(ax, data.items()):\n",
    "    curves = {k: [] for k in MODELS}\n",
    "    for _, lb, nrm in configs:\n",
    "        res = table(y, lb, nrm, \"single\")\n",
    "        for k in MODELS:\n",
    "            curves[k].append(res[k][0])\n",
    "    for k, v in curves.items():\n",
    "        a.plot(range(len(configs)), v, \"o-\", label=k)\n",
    "    a.set_xticks(range(len(configs)))\n",
    "    a.set_xticklabels([c[0] for c in configs], fontsize=7)\n",
    "    a.set_title(f\"{dname} data\"); a.set_ylabel(\"RMSE\")\n",
    "ax[0].legend(fontsize=8)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "1b7ebb68",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 9 - Error by horizon\n",
    "\n",
    "One averaged number hides the shape. Error must grow with horizon; where it does not, the model is\n",
    "leaning on seasonality rather than on the recent past."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 10,
   "id": "78a4b2ea",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-21T08:27:08.999556Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-21T08:27:08.998859Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-21T08:27:14.497328Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-21T08:27:14.494247Z"

   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",

       "<Figure size 1100x340 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"

   ],
   "source": [
    "X, Y = windows(data[\"seasonal\"], 96)\n",
    "tr = int(0.7 * len(X))\n",
    "mu, sd = X.mean(1, keepdims=True), X.std(1, keepdims=True) + 1e-6\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(11, 3.4))\n",
    "for name, fn in MODELS.items():\n",
    "    pred = fn((X[:tr] - mu[:tr]) / sd[:tr], (Y[:tr] - mu[:tr]) / sd[:tr], (X[tr:] - mu[tr:]) / sd[tr:])\n",
    "    pred = pred * sd[tr:] + mu[tr:]\n",
    "    plt.plot(np.sqrt(((pred - Y[tr:]) ** 2).mean(0)), label=name)\n",
    "plt.xlabel(\"horizon h\"); plt.ylabel(\"RMSE\"); plt.legend(fontsize=8)\n",
    "plt.title(\"Error vs. horizon \u2014 the curve a single averaged number hides\")\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "ada72cb6",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": "## What to take away\n\n| Observation in the numbers above | Lesson |\n| --- | --- |\n| DLinear and the plain linear model agree to three decimals | the decomposition is a reparameterisation of the same linear map \u2014 attributing a win to it would have been wrong |\n| At lookback 48 PatchMLP wins; at 96 and 336 the linear models win | the ranking is a property of method **\u00d7** protocol **\u00d7** data (Ch.7 L02). A paper reports one column |\n| Instance normalisation costs PatchMLP ~25 % on the regime data and the linear models nothing | preprocessing interacts with architecture; ablate it before believing any comparison |\n| Rolling origin: the linear/PatchMLP gap (~0.22) is the size of the fold spread (\u00b10.20) | a single split would have reported that gap as a result (Ch.1 L03) |\n| Scoring on the normalised scale compresses every difference | the metric's scale is a protocol choice too \u2014 check which one a paper reports |\n| Seasonal naive is beaten clearly, and is still the only free method here | always in the table (Ch.0 L02) |\n\n## Exercises\n\n1. Add **self-attention over the patch tokens** (a real PatchTST, in PyTorch). Does it change the\n   ranking more than instance normalisation did?\n2. Tune the lookback **per model** by inner validation, then re-run. How much of the apparent\n   difference survives?\n3. Add a Diebold-Mariano test (Ch.1 L03) on the per-window losses of the top two models. Is the gap\n   statistically distinguishable from zero?\n4. Swap the target for a 2D trajectory (predict $(x,y)$ jointly) and check whether the ranking\n   holds when the output is geometric (Ch.7 L03)."
  }
 ],
